report_name: New 'Prinz Eugen' Ransomware Written in Go Features Stealthy Extortion Tactics

threat_actors.name: Prinz Eugen
threat_actors.description: A new ransomware group that operates the Prinz Eugen ransomware. The group employs a double-extortion strategy, exfiltrating data to a leak site before encryption, and is known for not dropping a ransom note on victim systems. Initial targets are in the consumer, business, and financial services sectors.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of public data leaks.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Prinz Eugen ransomware encrypts files on victim systems using the ChaCha20-Poly1305 algorithm as its primary method of impact.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: As part of its double-extortion model, the Prinz Eugen group exfiltrates victim data to a data leak site before encrypting files locally.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Once executed, the Prinz Eugen ransomware recursively scans directories to find files for encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware operators use the command 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to delete volume shadow copies, hindering system recovery from local backups.

malware.name: Prinz Eugen Ransomware
malware.description: A new ransomware strain written in the Go programming language, designed for efficient, multi-threaded file encryption using the ChaCha20-Poly1305 algorithm. It is distinguished by its stealthy tactic of not dropping a ransom note and appends the '.prinzeugen' extension to encrypted files.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used to delete volume shadow copies with the command 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to prevent system recovery.

infrastructure.name: Prinz Eugen Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A data leak site used by the Prinz Eugen group to publish exfiltrated victim data as part of their double-extortion strategy.

indicators.name: Prinz Eugen Encrypted File Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Prinz Eugen Ransomware File Extension
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the '.prinzeugen' extension, which is indicative of the Prinz Eugen ransomware encrypting files.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/news/weekly-intelligence-report-26-jun-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.prinzeugen'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the .prinzeugen extension, a high-fidelity indicator of Prinz Eugen ransomware activity.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion Command
id: f8a7b6c5-d4e3-4f2a-1b09-c8d7e6f5a4b3
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/news/weekly-intelligence-report-26-jun-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' used to prevent system recovery.
